totalContractValue: $2,400 for the initial 12-month term (plus potential renewal cost increases)
recurringFees: $2,400/year billed annually in advance. No monthly billing option offered.
paymentSchedule: Full annual fee ($2,400) due at contract execution. No installment option. Late payments accrue 1.5% monthly interest.
lateFees: 1.5% per month (18% APR) on overdue balances. CloudBase may suspend access after 10 days past due.
penalties: Service suspension for non-payment. No cure period beyond 10 days for payment defaults.
refundTerms: All fees are non-refundable. Termination for convenience forfeits remaining prepaid term. No prorated refund on cancellation.
renewalCostChanges: CloudBase may increase fees on renewal with 60 days' notice. No cap on the percentage increase. Customer's only recourse is to terminate.

financialRedFlags.explanation: All $2,400 must be paid upfront at execution, and no refund is available if the service is inadequate or the contract is terminated early.
financialRedFlags.supportingQuote: All fees are non-refundable. Customer shall not be entitled to a refund of any prepaid fees upon termination for convenience.

financialRedFlags.explanation: CloudBase can raise prices on renewal with just 60 days' notice and no cap. A significant price increase forces Customer to choose between accepting the increase or losing the prepaid term.
financialRedFlags.supportingQuote: CloudBase reserves the right to modify fees upon renewal, provided Customer receives sixty (60) days' prior written notice.

financialRedFlags.explanation: 1.5% per month equals 18% APR — significantly above typical commercial late fee rates of 6–10% APR.
financialRedFlags.supportingQuote: Overdue amounts shall accrue interest at the rate of one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month.

financialRedFlags.explanation: CloudBase may suspend service after just 10 days of payment default, with no minimum notice window beyond the initial invoice.
financialRedFlags.supportingQuote: CloudBase may suspend Customer's access to the Service upon ten (10) days' written notice following a payment default.

citations.quote: Customer shall pay all fees in advance at the commencement of each Subscription Term. All fees are non-refundable.
citations.relevance: Establishes the non-refundable annual prepayment structure.

citations.quote: Overdue amounts shall accrue interest at the rate of one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month.
citations.relevance: 18% APR late fee is above standard commercial rates.

citations.quote: CloudBase reserves the right to modify fees upon renewal, provided Customer receives sixty (60) days' prior written notice.
citations.relevance: No cap on renewal price increases creates budget unpredictability.
